comment_text:
What observations are after this season:
1. Deandre Ayton is not the long‑term answer
Ayton has the tools — size, touch, mobility — but he’s never consistently shown the motor or physicality needed to anchor a championship defense.
The Lakers need a center who can:

Protect the rim

Rebound at a high level

Punish switches

Run the floor

He’s not a natural rim deterrent

Rebounding effort fluctuates

Offense is finesse‑heavy, not force‑heavy

the center needs to be a tone‑setter, not a passenger.

2. Laravia being below average
He’s a situational player — can shoot, can move the ball, but doesn’t tilt the floor.

Not a plus defender

Not a movement shooter

Not a creator

He’s a roster filler unless he takes a leap.

3. Rui Hachimura showing real value
 Rui’s value skyrockets when:

He’s aggressive as a scorer

He’s used as a mismatch forward

He’s one of the few Lakers who can create his own shot without a screen. That matters in the playoffs.

4. Addou looking like a real future piece
His flashes — length, defensive instincts, slashing — look like a modern wing archetype.
If he develops a reliable jumper, he becomes a real two‑way asset.

comment_text:
0.000001% of people know #30 scored 1.26 points per shot without freethrows that year.
AI first scoring title scored 0.88 points per shot without freethrows
Westbrook first scoring title scored 0.9 points per shot without freethrows
MJ first scoring title scored 0,96 points per shot without freethrows
Harden first scoring title scored 1,07 points per shot without freethrows
SGA first scoring title scored 1,13 points per shot without freethrows
All these guys got to the line a 9-12 times a game for their scoring title, without that theyd score 19-25 points and all of them were top 3 in FTa or FTm.
Steph wasnt even in the top 15 for FTa. Imagine if he ever got the whistle?
And no, its not because of his 50/50 three ball vs finishing/midrange. 
Young, Lillard, Harden have all had multiple 50/50 seasons that saw them get 8-12 freethrows a game across multiple seasons (8-12 respective to player).
And none of them gets double teamed, armbarred or bearhugged offball to add to the fouls. But hey.... freedom of movement is not something they call involving #30.
Slapping or punching at his fingertips just after release is also something they dont call for #30.

His smile hits just as hard as his crossovers, and the way he plays shows real passion and talent, like the game is just a part of him. comment_text: Chet absolutely choked during this series , he was a nonfactor...for him to be paid as a supermax player, so disappointing. With Jdub and Mitchell out with injuries ,that was his time to step up and he couldn't. Right now he still nothing more than a glorified role player. The front court has to be  revamped. Wemby basically punked the entire Thunder front court. They need  big wide bodies  to physically wear him down . Nobody on the Thunder's front court has the ability to make Wemby work on both ends of the court. Off-season changes are must  Dort is gone , he  has become a liability to team ,, some of the wings on this team should be traded for quality size and depth in the front court.  Imo the  main reason the Thunder has struggled ,SGA is not true play maker like a Chris Paul was.  Injuries decimated this squad, they never truly played any significant amount of time together to gain any  kind of chemistry. . Wiggins or Joe or both have to be traded for front court help. Bring back Caruso,  with J Mac ,along with Wallace . It will be interesting to see how Sorber comes back from injury.  He brings physicality and size and dependable rebounder. Thunder were  not a good rebounding team this season.. Chet needs to spend this off season working on his body to build muscle and add weight. 7'1  at 208 lbs is not going to cut it in the loaded  Western conference now.
